summary: The PR is a single coherent intent: hardening the gate-evidence mechanism against two edge cases — safeReaddir ENOTDIR handling in execution-state.js and buildEvidenceItem field-precedence in pr-manager.js — plus removal of the now-redundant {...extra, kind} workaround that the buildEvidenceItem fix makes unnecessary. All 9 files (2 source, 2 tests, story/architecture/spec docs, design-ssot registration) trace to one theme, no unrelated refactors or drive-by edits. The story explicitly excludes the other 4 salvage edits with stated security/contract and redundancy reasons, keeping different-risk changes out. Scope is appropriately sized — neither over-bundled nor in need of splitting.

inspection.summary: Inspected git diff origin/main --stat (9 files, 6 commits, one theme); the two source diffs; the story Scope/非目標 sections; design-ssot.json (one added root, no unrelated edits); the new test files.
inputs: git diff origin/main --stat; git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline

judgment_delta: Initial read (single robustness theme, small diff) leaned pass; after inspecting the actual diff, the Scope/非目標 sections, and confirming design-ssot and test additions touch nothing outside this story, pass held — the redundant-workaround removal is a legitimate consequence of the same fix (not scope creep), and the explicit exclusion of the 4 higher-risk salvage edits confirms deliberate single-intent boundaries.
